safest_plan.entry_basis: Top of Zero's buy zone, the 0.786+0.79 fib confluence at 148–150 (both 16 May and 2 Jun charts); a second, deeper buy sits at the 0.886 fib ~141–145 (estimated band off the chart's green zone, lower edge ~141.43)

targets.basis: TP1 — 0.786 fib, labelled on both Zero charts
TP2 — 0.705 fib, labelled on the 2 Jun chart

safest_plan.stop_basis: Daily close below 140, under the 0.886 fib / break-of-structure base (Zero's newest 2 Jun call; the older 16 May read used the 143.11 BOS level)

stance_by_source.summary: Buy the pullback into ~150, targets 196 then 211, but graded this setup 'weak' (4/10) with price slipping back to ~155 — conviction fading.
Buy the 148–150 pullback into the 0.886 fib / breaker, targets 196 (TP1) and 211 (TP2), stop on a close below 143.11; graded 'partial' (7/10).

conflicts: Both reads agree on direction (buy the dip to 148–150) but the newer 2 Jun read downgrades the setup to 'weak' (4/10) versus the older 16 May 'partial' (7/10) — Zero's own conviction is fading.
The stop level differs: the newer 2 Jun read uses a daily close below 140, the older 16 May read used 143.11 (the break-of-structure level); the more recent, lower 140 is used here per recency.
The chart shows a second, deeper buy zone at the 0.886 fib (~141–145) below the 148–150 zone that isn't in the headline entry text.
plain_english: Both of Zero's reads point the same way — this is a buy-on-a-dip idea, not a chase. He wants to buy Solana on a pullback into roughly $148–150 (with a possible deeper buy around $141–145), aiming for $196 and then $211. But his most recent read on 2 June marked the setup as weak, and the price near $155 hasn't actually reached the buy zone yet. Wait for a dip into $148–150 before buying anything, and abandon the idea if it closes a day below $140.
